# Shredded variant golden fixtures

Java-authored shredded variant Parquet files vendored from
[apache/parquet-testing](https://github.com/apache/parquet-testing/tree/master/shredded_variant),
generated from Iceberg Java's `TestVariantReaders`. They verify that the Iceberg
scanner reads externally-written shredded variant columns correctly.

Each `case-NNN.parquet` holds an `id` column and a shredded `var` variant column.
Each `case-NNN_row-R.variant.bin` is the canonical Java serialization (`metadata`
bytes followed by `value` bytes) of the variant expected at row `R`; a row with
no `.variant.bin` is a null variant.

A full survey of all 137 upstream cases against the arrow-go reader lives in
`go-notes/986-shredded-conformance-and-patches.md`. The cases vendored here are a
representative sample covering each distinct reader behaviour; the survey proves
the rest.

## Success cases (reassemble to the Java golden)

| Case | Upstream test | Path exercised |
|------|---------------|----------------|
| 001  | testSimpleArray | top-level shredded array |
| 002  | testEmptyArray | empty shredded array |
| 013  | testShreddedVariantPrimitives | shredded scalar primitive (int64) |
| 133  | testShreddedObjectMissingField | object field absent from `typed_value` (residual fallback) |
| 138  | testShreddedObjectMissingValueColumn | shredded object with no residual `value` column |
| 136  | testNestedArray | nested array |
| 135  | testNullArray | null variant value |
| 089  | testUnshreddedVariantsWithShreddedSchema | shredded schema, value in residual column (present JSON-null) |
| 083  | testMixedRecords | multi-record: a null row plus objects with scalar and nested-object fields |

## Error cases (reader must reject)

| Case | Upstream test | error_message |
|------|---------------|---------------|
| 040  | testArrayWithElementValueTypedValueConflict | conflicting value and typed_value |
| 042  | testValueAndTypedValueConflict | conflicting value and typed_value |
| 087  | testNonObjectWithNonNullShreddedFields | non-object value with shredded fields |
| 128  | testEmptyPartiallyShreddedObjectConflict | non-object value with shredded fields |
| 127  | testUnsignedInteger | unsupported shredded type (uint32) |
| 137  | testFixedLengthByteArray | unsupported shredded type (fixed_len_byte_array) |

Cases 040/042/087/128 are rejected by the scanner. Cases 127/137 are read as
`null` instead: arrow-go intentionally supports `uint32`/`fixed_size_binary`
`typed_value` (a superset of the Java reference), and these fixtures carry null
data, so the reassembled result is null. That divergence is arrow-go leniency,
not a bug - see the conformance note for the evidence.

## Invalid cases (spec permits error or read)

| Case | Upstream test | our reader |
|------|---------------|------------|
| 043  | testPartiallyShreddedObjectMissingFieldConflict | reads the value |
| 084  | testShreddedObjectWithOptionalFieldStructs | rejected at file conversion |

Run `./gen_fixtures.sh` to (re)download the fixtures. It pins a specific upstream
commit for reproducibility and rewrites the generated links below.
